symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden 'pop' and severe lower back pain during a manual lift/transfer

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute mechanical lumbosacral strain / muscle spasm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A discrete overload event with immediate pain is the classic mechanism of acute muscular/ligamentous lumbar strain.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Cauda equina syndrome / acute nerve-root compression
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An acute lifting injury with functional collapse mandates a cauda equina screen even when the mechanism looks purely muscular.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral lumbar muscle spasm with complete inability to weight-bear

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute mechanical lumbosacral strain / muscle spasm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Protective bilateral paraspinal spasm producing functional lock-up is typical of a severe acute strain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cauda equina screen negative — no saddle anaesthesia, no sphincter disturbance, intact foot/toe movement and sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Cauda equina syndrome / acute nerve-root compression
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative patient-reportable red flags lower the probability of cauda equina but cannot fully exclude it without an in-person neurological exam.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mechanical pain pattern — worse with movement, eased by lying still, no colic, no groin radiation, no fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute mechanical lumbosacral strain / muscle spasm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Movement-provoked, rest-eased pain without colic, groin radiation or fever fits a mechanical strain and argues against renal colic or infection.
Absence of colicky, groin-radiating flank pain argues against a renal stone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic carer exhaustion, sleep deprivation and self-healthcare neglect (>3 years without own GP review)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sustained unsupported caregiving with exhaustion and neglected self-care is the substrate for carer role strain / adjustment disorder and for repetitive manual-handling injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild tachycardia and diaphoresis (source-measured HR 102, BP 140/88) — pain-driven autonomic response

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute mechanical lumbosacral strain / muscle spasm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia and raised BP are attributable to severe pain; sealed clinician measurement — the AI infers distress from the patient's report, not a measured vital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Neurological examination — straight-leg-raise negative bilaterally, sensation intact, gross toe/ankle movement intact, no midline bony tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Cauda equina syndrome / acute nerve-root compression
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The in-person neurological exam is what allowed the source clinician to exclude cord/root compromise — precisely the assessment a telehealth service cannot perform, which is why in-person review is required.
Absence of midline bony tenderness (muscular rather than bony pain) argues against a compression fracture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person neurological examination (perianal sensation, anal tone, lower-limb power/reflexes, straight-leg-raise)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Completes the cauda equina exclusion that cannot be done remotely; the pivotal same-day in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Lumbar spine imaging (MRI) for cauda equina
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not first-line and not AI-orderable via telehealth; reserved for the in-person assessor only if red flags emerge on examination — the AI should not over-order imaging remotely.
